Hey Carl-Johannes,

The Block RAM behavior divergence you reported between ghdl and Vivado's simulator is a really practical problem. BRAM inference is already vendor-specific in synthesis, but when the simulators disagree on read-during-write behavior, you can't trust your testbench results to predict what the hardware will do.

Did you end up having to write your BRAM descriptions differently to get consistent behavior across both simulators, or was there a ghdl-side fix?

outreach_subject: Bootstrap circuit for the MMC
outreach_message:
Bootstrap gate drive is one of those things that seems simple until you have to handle the refresh timing across dozens of submodules in a modular multilevel converter — if the high-side capacitor droops during a long ON-state, the MOSFET gate voltage sags and you get shoot-through. The OwnTech foundation building open-source power electronics at this level is rare. Are you targeting a specific switching frequency range where the bootstrap cap sizing becomes the limiting constraint, or is the design flexible enough to cover both low-frequency MMC operation and higher-frequency PWM?

outreach_subject: NRZ timing in the WS2812B driver
outreach_message:

What led you to clock-gate the RAM originally — was it an attempt at power optimization, and was the error a DRC failure or a timing closure issue?

Hey Shwetank,

Gate-level simulation mismatches in your RISC-V RTL-to-GDSII flow are one of the hardest problems to debug. The design passes RTL simulation, synthesis looks clean, and then GLS reveals behavior differences that trace back to timing, optimization, or uninitialized state.

Curious what the mismatch turned out to be in your case. Was it a timing-related issue that only showed up post-synthesis, or something the synthesis tool optimized away that the RTL was relying on?

outreach_subject: placement ignoring PDN stripes in OpenROAD
outreach_message:
Your issue about placement ignoring PDN stripes in OpenROAD points to a frustrating gap between the power planning and placement stages. If the placer doesn't respect power grid geometry, you end up with cells on top of stripes and DRC nightmares downstream.

Did the issue turn out to be a missing constraint handoff between the PDN generator and the placer, or something deeper in how OpenROAD models obstructions?

outreach_subject: get_ports curly brackets in yosys-f4pga
outreach_message:
Hey Robert,

The curly brackets issue in get_ports for the yosys-f4pga plugins is one of those SDC compatibility problems that makes open source EDA adoption harder than it should be. Antmicro's fingerprints are all over the CHIPS Alliance tooling, so you're probably seeing these interop issues more than anyone.

Curious how close the yosys-f4pga SDC support is to what teams actually need for production designs. Is it mostly edge cases like bracket parsing, or are there bigger gaps in constraint coverage?

Severin
